hero.question: US x Iran ceasefire extended by April 21, 2026?
hero.prob_take: The crowd is pricing a 66% shot at a US-Iran ceasefire extension by April 2026, but that 18-point drop in 24 hours signals something shifted: either new escalation intel hit, or the market finally priced in how hard it is to lock in a deal when both sides are still testing each other's red lines. The real tell is volume at $846K, which is fat enough to mean serious money moved, not noise, so watch whether the next headline is a diplomatic opening or another proxy strike, because 66% assumes deal momentum we don't actually have yet.

daily_take.headline: Iran Ceasefire Odds Crumble 18 Points. Smart Money Sees Escalation Risk.
daily_take.deck: A massive 18-point drop in 24 hours signals real traders are repositioning hard on US-Iran tensions. The market priced ceasefire extension at 66%, but that assumed stability we no longer have. Sharp bettors are already shorting the optimistic scenario, which means either buying the dip if you see a diplomatic off-ramp, or doubling down on the bearish thesis that tensions keep ratcheting.
